prompt:
Our community tool-shed app finally turned nullable reference types on for the lending library project (it was the last one in the solution) and the build now fails, since we compile with warnings as errors. The dump:

    LendingShed.cs(6,19): error CS8618: Non-nullable field 'Id' must contain a non-null value when exiting constructor.
    LendingShed.cs(7,19): error CS8618: Non-nullable field 'Name' must contain a non-null value when exiting constructor.
    LendingShed.cs(8,19): error CS8618: Non-nullable field 'Category' must contain a non-null value when exiting constructor.
    LendingShed.cs(9,19): error CS8618: Non-nullable field 'Notes' must contain a non-null value when exiting constructor.
    LendingShed.cs(15,19): error CS8618: Non-nullable field 'ToolId' must contain a non-null value when exiting constructor.
    LendingShed.cs(16,19): error CS8618: Non-nullable field 'Member' must contain a non-null value when exiting constructor.
    LendingShed.cs(18,19): error CS8618: Non-nullable field 'ReturnedNote' must contain a non-null value when exiting constructor.
    LendingShed.cs(26,38): error CS8625: Cannot convert null literal to non-nullable reference type.
    LendingShed.cs(37,16): error CS8603: Possible null reference return.
    LendingShed.cs(50,16): error CS8603: Possible null reference return.
    LendingShedTests.cs(111,68): error CS8625: Cannot convert null literal to non-nullable reference type.

I want a real annotation pass, not a sea of exclamation marks — the null-forgiving operator (!) is banned in this codebase, full stop. Think about which nulls are honest and which are contract: a lookup that misses hands back nothing, a donated tool doesn't have to have notes, a loan has no returned-note while it's still out, and the out-list filter takes nothing to mean everyone. Meanwhile the hard errors (checking out an unknown tool, double checkout, returning something that isn't out) stay hard errors with the exact messages the tests pin. Don't change what the shed does — the xunit suite is the contract. Hands off LendingShedTests.cs, ToolLend.csproj and .gitignore; `dotnet test` green with zero warnings is done.
notes: 5W4 item 4: -warnaserror nullable retrofit without '!'. Authoring toolchain: dotnet SDK 10.0.302, xunit 2.9.3 (same pinned package set as the wave-4 csharp seeds). The gate is <TreatWarningsAsErrors> in the protected csproj (per-catalog '-warnaserror'), so plain dotnet test is the single verify command. Pre-fix: 11 nullable diagnostics — CS8618 x7 (Tool.Id/Name/Category/Notes, Loan.ToolId/Member/ReturnedNote), CS8603 x2 (Find and ActiveLoan return null on miss), CS8625 x2 (a `= null` field initializer, and one in the FROZEN tests where OutList(null) is called, which forces the param to become string? — the catalog's 'params' leg). Runtime semantics are already correct pre-fix (all null guards exist; the warnings are declaration-level), so the retrofit is purely deciding the null contract: required members vs string? per field (tests always set Id/Name/Category via object initializers but omit Notes; Loan is built internally), Tool?/Loan? returns, string? filter param, string? note property. Reference uses required + '?' annotations only; probed that xunit 2.9.3 Assert.NotNull carries [NotNull] so the frozen tests' deref-after-NotNull compiles warning-free — no '!' needed anywhere, keeping the prompt ban honest. Teeth: null-path contracts pinned (Find miss null, Notes null, LastReturnNote null before first return, OutList(null) = all open loans), exact hard-error types+messages, board lines, due-date maths, re-checkout after return. Domain check: rs-toolshed is a rust build seed about a different surface; csharp-kennelcard/gearrepo etc. don't cover lending flows — no re-skin.
